name: Top 5 Buzzboard Alternatives for SMB Leads
description:
__POSSIBLE_UNTRANSLATED__

If your agency needs a constant flow of SMB and local business leads, chances are Buzzboard has been part of your toolkit at some stage. It’s a familiar starting point. But as your agency’s reach expands, the limits of your tools reveal themselves. Your team needs richer local digital intelligence, sharper filters, quicker qualification, and a platform that helps reps pitch smarter without juggling multiple tools.

When your current software can’t support that next level of growth or when the cost per lead stops making sense for an SMB-focused model, it becomes clear that it’s time to look elsewhere. This guide walks you through the five best Buzzboard alternatives for SMB lead generation and highlights the essential features modern agencies should expect in a prospecting platform.

TL;DR

  • Buzzboard is fine for basic market intelligence and digital signals. But it has limited local presence. Agencies eventually outgrow it because the data is often incomplete, inaccurate, or too shallow for modern SMB prospecting.
  • ​Essential Alternative Features:
    • ​Deep local audit
    • ​Precision filtering
    • ​AI pitching
    • ​Workflow integration
    • ​SMB pricing
  • ​Top 5 Alternatives at a Glance
    • ​Synup: Best for marketing agencies needing extensive data points and deep local digital audits. It’s an all-in-one OS for SMB prospecting and client management. 
    • ​ZoomInfo: Best for enterprise B2B sales; offers high contact accuracy for corporate decision-makers.
    • ​Apollo: Best for affordable B2B lead gen; combines data with an outreach sequencing tool.
    • ​Openmart: Best for bulk data extraction from Google Maps; useful for large top-of-funnel lists.
    • ​Limeleads: Best for US-based B2B leads; features high email accuracy with a pay-for-verified model.

Why Switch from Buzzboard?

Plenty of agencies start with Buzzboard because it does the basics well: a quick snapshot of a business, the basic market intelligence and digital signals, and enough data to start an outbound list. But once you’re growing and your targets increase, those audits start slowing you down.

Say you’re running a 10-person agency, and you’ve set a realistic monthly target of 20 new client locations. That’s one new client every 1.5 days. To hit that comfortably, your reps need 60–80 solid outbound conversations from qualified prospects each month.

Here are some bottlenecks a lot of agencies hit with Buzzboard:

Data Accuracy 

A few glances at customer reviews online, and you'd notice something quite common with Buzzboard: inaccurate data 

One customer shared a simple but telling example. They were standing in a neighborhood full of Asian restaurants and tried searching for those within a 5-mile radius. Instead of the ten or more spots they already knew were there, the software returned only two results…and neither was even close. One was Italian, and the other was a seafood place.

Source: G2

The Time Problem

Most reps spend 35 to 45 minutes researching a single SMB prospect when they don’t have a tool that goes deep. They jump between:

  • Google Maps
  • The business’s website
  • Facebook/Instagram
  • Review sites
  • Directory listings
  • Competitor examples
  • Tech lookup tools

By the time they’re done, they’ve forgotten half the details and still haven’t uncovered anything specific enough to build a sharp pitch.

If each rep researches 10 prospects a day, that’s 6 hours burned on manual digging, time that should’ve gone into calls, emails, or follow-ups.

Multiply that by even 4 reps, and suddenly your team is blowing 100+ hours each week just trying to figure out what’s wrong with each business.

The Cost Problem

Agencies don’t usually calculate their “research cost per lead,” but you should.

Let’s run a quick mental example:

  • Rep hourly cost (fully loaded): $28/hour
  • Manual research per lead: 40 minutes
  • Cost per researched lead: roughly $19 per lead
  • Leads needed per month (to hit 20 new clients at a 10 to 15% close rate): 120 to 150 leads

That means your research overhead alone lands somewhere around:

120 leads x $19 = $2,280 

or 

150 leads x $19 = $2,850

This doesn’t even include outreach, meetings, proposals, admin work, or the inevitable “no-shows.”

Now imagine halving that research time or eliminating it entirely with automated audits. Your cost per lead drops immediately, and your sales pipeline moves faster without adding more staff.

What Features to Look for in a Buzzboard Alternative?

An effective alternative must go beyond a contact list. It should help your team move through research, qualification, and pitch creation without bouncing between multiple apps.

  1. Deep Local Digital Auditing

You want data that goes far beyond firmographics. The platform should identify hyper-specific issues, such as missing booking widgets, mismatched name/address/phone data across directories, broken mobile layouts, or a low review rating compared with local competitors. 

  1. Precision Filtering Based on Needs 

Instead of filtering by industry and employee count, you need filters like outdated technology, missing analytics tools, unclaimed listings, inactive social profiles, or a poor mobile score. These filters help you spot high-intent prospects. 

  1. AI-Enhanced Pitch Strategy and Responder 

Make sure you choose a tool that uses artificial intelligence to do the work for you, i.e., respond to leads, interpret data, etc. For instance, Synup surfaces the most important digital gaps and instantly turns them into a pitch angle your reps can use. It also prioritizes leads most likely to need your services.

  1. Agency Workflow Integration 

Your prospecting tool should connect to actual delivery. If a lead becomes a client, you should not need a second platform to manage onboarding, tasks, invoicing, and reporting. This creates a cleaner path from discovery to revenue.

‍
